rounds.blurb: RMS Titanic departs Southampton on her maiden — and only — voyage.
rounds.story: Four days after this Southampton departure, Titanic struck an iceberg and sank; more than 1,500 of the roughly 2,200 aboard died. Stuart's photograph shows her at the moment she still represented the future of ocean travel.

rounds.blurb: "Tear down this wall" — Ronald Reagan speaks at the Brandenburg Gate in West Berlin.
rounds.credit: White House Photographic Office / DPLA — public domain
rounds.story: Reagan's advisers cut the line 'tear down this wall' from draft after draft as too provocative; he kept putting it back. Twenty-nine months later, Berliners were dancing on top of it.

rounds.blurb: Voyager 2 leaves Earth aboard a Titan IIIE — carrying the Golden Record toward interstellar space.
rounds.story: Voyager 2 actually launched sixteen days before Voyager 1, on a slower path that let it visit all four outer planets — an alignment that comes once every 176 years. Nearly five decades on, it still calls home from interstellar space.

rounds.blurb: Elvis Presley meets Richard Nixon in the Oval Office — the National Archives' most-requested photograph.
rounds.credit: Ollie Atkins / White House — public domain
rounds.story: Presley arrived unannounced at the White House gate with a handwritten letter asking to be made a federal agent-at-large in the war on drugs. Nixon gave him the badge — and the resulting photo became the National Archives' most requested item, ahead of the Constitution.

rounds.blurb: The International Space Station begins: the Unity node rides in Endeavour's payload bay before mating with Zarya.
rounds.story: Endeavour's crew mated the American Unity node to Russia's Zarya module, the station's first two pieces. The ISS has been continuously occupied since 2000 — the longest unbroken human presence off Earth.
